---
title: "Fitting a GLM-HMM to Matching Pennies Data"
---

A GLM-HMM (Generalized Linear Model -- Hidden Markov Model) assumes that on
each trial the agent occupies one of $K$ latent states, each with its own
logistic-regression weights mapping recent history to the probability of
choosing Right.  The model is fit with EM via the
[`ssm`](https://github.com/lindermanlab/ssm) library.

We use the same feature set as the mouse/human analyses in `matchingp`:

| Feature | Encoding |
|---|---|
| **bias** | constant 1 |
| **PrevRewardedDir** | previous choice direction (+1 R, −1 L) if rewarded, else 0 |
| **PrevUnrewardedDir** | previous choice direction if unrewarded, else 0 |

### Interactive demo

The widget below simulates a 3-state GLM-HMM.  Each state has its own
probability of choosing Right, and on each trial the model may stay in its
current state or switch to another.  Step through trials and watch how the
hidden state drives choice behaviour.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof pR0 = Inputs.range([0, 1], {value: 0.15, step: 0.01, label: "P(R) — Left-biased state"})
viewof pR1 = Inputs.range([0, 1], {value: 0.85, step: 0.01, label: "P(R) — Right-biased state"})
viewof pR2 = Inputs.range([0, 1], {value: 0.50, step: 0.01, label: "P(R) — Stochastic state"})
viewof stickiness = Inputs.range([0.5, 0.99], {value: 0.90, step: 0.01, label: "Self-transition prob."})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

mutable hmmState = ({
  currentK: 0, history: [],
  _stepN: 0, _step10N: 0, _backN: 0, _resetN: 0
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof hmmBackBtn = Inputs.button("Back")
viewof hmmStepBtn = Inputs.button("Next Trial")
viewof hmmStep10Btn = Inputs.button("+10 Trials")
viewof hmmResetBtn = Inputs.button("Reset")
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

html`<style>
  .hmm-demo button {
    padding: 6px 18px;
    font-size: 13px;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: white;
    border: none;
    border-radius: 6px;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .hmm-demo form:nth-child(1) button { background: #888; }
  .hmm-demo form:nth-child(2) button { background: #2A9DF4; }
  .hmm-demo form:nth-child(3) button { background: #6366F1; }
  .hmm-demo form:nth-child(4) button { background: #888; }
</style>
<div class="hmm-demo" style="display:flex; gap:8px; margin-bottom:8px;">
  ${viewof hmmBackBtn}
  ${viewof hmmStepBtn}
  ${viewof hmmStep10Btn}
  ${viewof hmmResetBtn}
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

stateNames = ["Left-biased", "Right-biased", "Stochastic"]
stateColors = ["#2A9DF4", "#F45B69", "#333333"]
statePRs = [pR0, pR1, pR2]

function hmmRunStep(s) {
  const pRs = statePRs;
  const k = s.currentK;
  const pR = pRs[k];
  const chooseRight = Math.random() < pR;
  const choice = chooseRight ? "R" : "L";

  // Transition: stay with prob stickiness, else uniform over the other two
  let nextK = k;
  if (Math.random() >= stickiness) {
    const others = [0, 1, 2].filter(x => x !== k);
    nextK = others[Math.floor(Math.random() * others.length)];
  }

  const entry = ({
    trial: s.history.length + 1,
    state: k, nextState: nextK,
    pR, choice,
  });
  return ({currentK: nextK, entry});
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

{
  // Back
  const s0 = hmmState;
  if (hmmBackBtn > s0._backN && s0.history.length > 0) {
    const last = s0.history[s0.history.length - 1];
    mutable hmmState = ({
      currentK: last.state,
      history: s0.history.slice(0, -1),
      _stepN: s0._stepN, _step10N: s0._step10N,
      _backN: hmmBackBtn, _resetN: s0._resetN
    });
  } else if (hmmBackBtn > s0._backN) {
    mutable hmmState = ({...s0, _backN: hmmBackBtn});
  }

  // Single step
  const s1 = hmmState;
  if (hmmStepBtn > s1._stepN) {
    const r = hmmRunStep(s1);
    mutable hmmState = ({
      currentK: r.currentK,
      history: [...s1.history, r.entry],
      _stepN: hmmStepBtn, _step10N: s1._step10N,
      _backN: s1._backN, _resetN: s1._resetN
    });
  }

  // +10 steps
  const s2 = hmmState;
  if (hmmStep10Btn > s2._step10N) {
    let cur = ({currentK: s2.currentK, history: [...s2.history],
                _stepN: s2._stepN, _step10N: s2._step10N,
                _backN: s2._backN, _resetN: s2._resetN});
    for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
      const r = hmmRunStep(cur);
      cur = ({...cur, currentK: r.currentK, history: [...cur.history, r.entry]});
    }
    mutable hmmState = ({...cur, _stepN: s2._stepN, _step10N: hmmStep10Btn,
                         _backN: s2._backN, _resetN: s2._resetN});
  }

  // Reset
  const s3 = hmmState;
  if (hmmResetBtn > s3._resetN) {
    mutable hmmState = ({
      currentK: 0, history: [],
      _stepN: hmmStepBtn, _step10N: hmmStep10Btn,
      _backN: hmmBackBtn, _resetN: hmmResetBtn
    });
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const w = Math.min(width, 700);
  const h = 340;
  const margin = ({top: 15, right: 20, bottom: 15, left: 20});
  const pw = w - margin.left - margin.right;
  const currentK = hmmState.currentK;
  const switchP = (1 - stickiness) / 2;

  const svg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${w} ${h}`)
    .attr("width", w).attr("height", h)
    .style("font-family", "Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif")
    .style("max-width", "100%");

  // Triangle layout: 0=Left-biased (top-left), 1=Right-biased (top-right), 2=Stochastic (bottom-center)
  const nodeX = [margin.left + pw * 0.22, margin.left + pw * 0.78, margin.left + pw * 0.5];
  const nodeY = [70, 70, 195];
  const radius = 42;

  // Arrowhead marker
  svg.append("defs").append("marker")
    .attr("id", "arrowhead").attr("viewBox", "0 0 10 10")
    .attr("refX", 8).attr("refY", 5)
    .attr("markerWidth", 6).attr("markerHeight", 6)
    .attr("orient", "auto-start-reverse")
    .append("path").attr("d", "M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z").attr("fill", "#aaa");

  // Draw transition arrows between each pair of states
  const pairs = [[0,1],[1,0],[0,2],[2,0],[1,2],[2,1]];
  for (const [from, to] of pairs) {
    const dx = nodeX[to] - nodeX[from];
    const dy = nodeY[to] - nodeY[from];
    const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
    const nx = dx / dist;
    const ny = dy / dist;

    // Start/end at circle edge
    const x1 = nodeX[from] + nx * (radius + 4);
    const y1 = nodeY[from] + ny * (radius + 4);
    const x2 = nodeX[to] - nx * (radius + 8);
    const y2 = nodeY[to] - ny * (radius + 8);

    // Offset perpendicular so forward/reverse arrows don't overlap
    const perpX = -ny * 8;
    const perpY = nx * 8;

    svg.append("line")
      .attr("x1", x1 + perpX).attr("y1", y1 + perpY)
      .attr("x2", x2 + perpX).attr("y2", y2 + perpY)
      .attr("stroke", "#bbb").attr("stroke-width", 1.2)
      .attr("marker-end", "url(#arrowhead)");

    // Label at midpoint, offset outward
    const mx = (x1 + x2) / 2 + perpX * 1.8;
    const my = (y1 + y2) / 2 + perpY * 1.8;
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", mx).attr("y", my + 4)
      .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 10).attr("fill", "#999")
      .text(switchP.toFixed(2));
  }

  // Self-transition loops
  const loopAngles = [-Math.PI / 2, -Math.PI / 2, Math.PI / 2];
  for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {
    const ang = loopAngles[k];
    const lx1 = nodeX[k] + Math.cos(ang - 0.4) * (radius + 2);
    const ly1 = nodeY[k] + Math.sin(ang - 0.4) * (radius + 2);
    const lx2 = nodeX[k] + Math.cos(ang + 0.4) * (radius + 2);
    const ly2 = nodeY[k] + Math.sin(ang + 0.4) * (radius + 2);
    const cpDist = 40;
    const cpx = nodeX[k] + Math.cos(ang) * (radius + cpDist);
    const cpy = nodeY[k] + Math.sin(ang) * (radius + cpDist);

    svg.append("path")
      .attr("d", `M${lx1},${ly1} Q${cpx},${cpy} ${lx2},${ly2}`)
      .attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#bbb").attr("stroke-width", 1.2)
      .attr("marker-end", "url(#arrowhead)");

    const labelDist = radius + cpDist + 4;
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", nodeX[k] + Math.cos(ang) * labelDist)
      .attr("y", nodeY[k] + Math.sin(ang) * labelDist + 4)
      .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 10).attr("fill", "#999")
      .text(stickiness.toFixed(2));
  }

  // State circles and labels
  for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {
    svg.append("circle")
      .attr("cx", nodeX[k]).attr("cy", nodeY[k]).attr("r", radius)
      .attr("fill", k === currentK ? stateColors[k] : "white")
      .attr("stroke", stateColors[k])
      .attr("stroke-width", k === currentK ? 3 : 2)
      .attr("opacity", k === currentK ? 0.2 : 1);
    if (k === currentK) {
      svg.append("circle")
        .attr("cx", nodeX[k]).attr("cy", nodeY[k]).attr("r", radius)
        .attr("fill", "none")
        .attr("stroke", stateColors[k]).attr("stroke-width", 3);
    }
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", nodeX[k]).attr("y", nodeY[k] - 6)
      .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 11)
      .attr("font-weight", 600)
      .attr("fill", k === currentK ? stateColors[k] : "#555")
      .text(stateNames[k]);
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", nodeX[k]).attr("y", nodeY[k] + 14)
      .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 12)
      .attr("fill", k === currentK ? stateColors[k] : "#888")
      .text(`P(R) = ${statePRs[k].toFixed(2)}`);
  }

  // Last trial info
  const hist = hmmState.history;
  const infoY = 265;
  if (hist.length > 0) {
    const last = hist[hist.length - 1];
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", w / 2).attr("y", infoY)
      .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 13).attr("fill", "#333")
      .text(`Trial ${last.trial}: chose ${last.choice} from ${stateNames[last.state]} state`);
    if (last.state !== last.nextState) {
      svg.append("text")
        .attr("x", w / 2).attr("y", infoY + 20)
        .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 12).attr("fill", "#F59E0B")
        .attr("font-weight", 600)
        .text(`→ switched to ${stateNames[last.nextState]}`);
    }
  } else {
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", w / 2).attr("y", infoY)
      .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 13)
      .attr("fill", "#999").attr("font-style", "italic")
      .text("Press Next Trial to begin.");
  }

  // State occupancy bar
  if (hist.length > 0) {
    const barY = 305;
    const barH = 14;
    const totalT = hist.length;
    let xOff = margin.left;
    for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {
      const count = hist.filter(d => d.state === k).length;
      const frac = count / totalT;
      const bw = frac * pw;
      if (bw > 0) {
        svg.append("rect")
          .attr("x", xOff).attr("y", barY)
          .attr("width", bw).attr("height", barH)
          .attr("fill", stateColors[k]).attr("opacity", 0.7).attr("rx", 2);
        if (frac > 0.08) {
          svg.append("text")
            .attr("x", xOff + bw / 2).attr("y", barY + barH / 2 + 4)
            .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 10)
            .attr("fill", "white").attr("font-weight", 600)
            .text(`${Math.round(frac * 100)}%`);
        }
        xOff += bw;
      }
    }
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", margin.left).attr("y", barY - 5)
      .attr("font-size", 10).attr("fill", "#888")
      .text("State occupancy:");
  }

  return svg.node();
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const recent = hmmState.history.slice(-60);
  if (recent.length === 0) return html``;

  const w = Math.min(width, 700);
  const h = 220;
  const margin = ({top: 10, right: 15, bottom: 60, left: 50});
  const pw = w - margin.left - margin.right;
  const ph = h - margin.top - margin.bottom;

  const svg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${w} ${h}`)
    .attr("width", w).attr("height", h)
    .style("font-family", "Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif")
    .style("max-width", "100%");

  const trials = recent.map(d => d.trial);
  const sx = d3.scaleLinear()
    .domain([d3.min(trials) - 0.5, d3.max(trials) + 0.5])
    .range([margin.left, margin.left + pw]);
  const choiceY = ({R: margin.top + 10, L: margin.top + ph - 10});

  // State shading
  let blockStart = recent[0].trial;
  let blockK = recent[0].state;
  for (let i = 1; i <= recent.length; i++) {
    const cur = i < recent.length ? recent[i] : null;
    if (cur === null || cur.state !== blockK) {
      const x1 = sx(blockStart - 0.5);
      const x2 = sx((cur ? cur.trial : recent[recent.length - 1].trial + 1) - 0.5);
      svg.append("rect")
        .attr("x", x1).attr("y", margin.top)
        .attr("width", x2 - x1).attr("height", ph)
        .attr("fill", stateColors[blockK]).attr("opacity", 0.1);
      if (cur) { blockStart = cur.trial; blockK = cur.state; }
    }
  }

  // Center line
  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", margin.left).attr("x2", margin.left + pw)
    .attr("y1", margin.top + ph / 2).attr("y2", margin.top + ph / 2)
    .attr("stroke", "#ddd").attr("stroke-dasharray", "3,3");

  // Choice ticks
  for (const d of recent) {
    const color = d.choice === "R" ? "#F45B69" : "#2A9DF4";
    const y1 = margin.top + ph / 2;
    const y2 = d.choice === "R" ? margin.top + ph - 5 : margin.top + 5;
    svg.append("line")
      .attr("x1", sx(d.trial)).attr("x2", sx(d.trial))
      .attr("y1", y1).attr("y2", y2)
      .attr("stroke", color).attr("stroke-width", 1.5).attr("opacity", 0.8);
  }

  // Labels
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", margin.left - 6).attr("y", margin.top + 12)
    .attr("text-anchor", "end").attr("font-size", 11)
    .attr("fill", "#2A9DF4").attr("font-weight", 600).text("L");
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", margin.left - 6).attr("y", margin.top + ph - 4)
    .attr("text-anchor", "end").attr("font-size", 11)
    .attr("fill", "#F45B69").attr("font-weight", 600).text("R");

  // X-axis
  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", margin.left).attr("x2", margin.left + pw)
    .attr("y1", margin.top + ph).attr("y2", margin.top + ph)
    .attr("stroke", "#333");
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", margin.left + pw / 2).attr("y", margin.top + ph + 18)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 11).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text("Trial");

  // Legend below plot
  const lgY = h - 22;
  const lg = svg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(${margin.left}, ${lgY})`);
  for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {
    const xOff = k * 160;
    lg.append("rect").attr("x", xOff).attr("y", -7).attr("width", 14).attr("height", 14)
      .attr("fill", stateColors[k]).attr("opacity", 0.25).attr("rx", 2);
    lg.append("text").attr("x", xOff + 20).attr("y", 5)
      .attr("font-size", 12).attr("fill", "#555").text(stateNames[k]);
  }

  return svg.node();
}
```

---

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import ssm
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "notebooks"))
from style import (apply_style, plot_choices, CHOICE_COLORS,
                   STRATEGY_COLORS, STATE_COLORS)
apply_style()
```

## Load synthetic data

```{python}
df = pd.read_csv("data/synthetic_mp.csv")
print(f"{len(df)} trials, {df['session_code'].nunique()} sessions, "
      f"strategies: {df['strategy'].unique().tolist()}")
```

## Build GLM-HMM inputs

The function below mirrors `make_hmm_glm_inputs` from
`matchingp.modeling.train` but is self-contained.

```{python}
def make_glmhmm_inputs(df):
    """Convert a trial-level DataFrame into the list-of-sessions format
    expected by ssm.HMM.fit().

    Returns
    -------
    true_choices : list of (T, 1) int arrays   -- 0=L, 1=R
    inpts        : list of (T, 3) float arrays  -- [bias, prevRewDir, prevUnrewDir]
    session_codes : list of str
    """
    true_choices, inpts, session_codes = [], [], []
    for sess, sdf in df.groupby("session_code"):
        agent_choice = (sdf["choice"].values == "R").astype(float)
        reward = (sdf["reward"].values > 0).astype(float)
        T = len(agent_choice)

        bias = np.ones(T)

        prev_dir = np.zeros(T)
        prev_dir[1:] = np.where(agent_choice[:-1] == 1, 1, -1)

        prev_reward = np.zeros(T)
        prev_reward[1:] = reward[:-1]

        prev_rewarded_dir = prev_dir * prev_reward
        prev_unrewarded_dir = prev_dir * (1 - prev_reward)

        X = np.column_stack([bias, prev_rewarded_dir, prev_unrewarded_dir])
        y = agent_choice.astype(np.int64).reshape(-1, 1)

        true_choices.append(y)
        inpts.append(X)
        session_codes.append(sess)

    return true_choices, inpts, session_codes


true_choices, inpts, session_codes = make_glmhmm_inputs(df)
print(f"Sessions: {len(true_choices)}, "
      f"input dim: {inpts[0].shape[1]}, "
      f"total trials: {sum(len(y) for y in true_choices)}")
```

## Fit models with varying numbers of states

```{python}
# -- Sweep configuration --------------------------------------------------
num_states_list = [1, 2, 3, 4]  # candidate numbers of hidden states (K)
obs_dim = 1                      # single binary observation per trial (L/R)
input_dim = inpts[0].shape[1]   # number of GLM regressors (bias + history features)
num_categories = 2               # binary choice: Left or Right

models = {}
log_likelihoods = {}

for K in num_states_list:
    glmhmm = ssm.HMM(
        K,                                # number of hidden states
        obs_dim,                          # observation dimensionality
        input_dim,                        # number of input (design-matrix) columns
        observations="input_driven_obs",  # GLM emission model (logistic regression per state)
        observation_kwargs=dict(
            C=num_categories,             # number of choice categories
            prior_sigma=2,                # std of Gaussian prior on GLM weights (regularisation)
        ),
        transition_kwargs=dict(
            alpha=1,                      # Dirichlet concentration for transition rows
            kappa=0,                      # extra self-transition weight (0 = no stickiness bias)
        ),
        transitions="sticky",            # sticky-HMM transition model
    )

    # Fit via Expectation-Maximisation
    # true_choices: list of (T_session, 1) arrays — observed choices per session
    # inpts:        list of (T_session, input_dim) arrays — design matrices per session
    fit_ll = glmhmm.fit(true_choices, inputs=inpts, method="em",
                        num_iters=200,    # maximum EM iterations
                        tolerance=1e-3)   # convergence threshold (ΔLL)

    # Normalise final log-likelihood to bits per trial for comparability
    total_trials = sum(len(y) for y in true_choices)
    ll_bits = fit_ll[-1] / (total_trials * np.log(2))
    models[K] = glmhmm
    log_likelihoods[K] = ll_bits
    print(f"K={K}: LL = {ll_bits:.4f} bits/trial  ({len(fit_ll)} EM iters)")
```

## Model comparison

```{python}
#| label: fig-model-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Log-likelihood (bits/trial) vs number of HMM states."
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 3))
ax.plot(list(log_likelihoods.keys()), list(log_likelihoods.values()),
        "o-", color="#2A9DF4", markersize=6)
ax.set_xlabel("Number of states K")
ax.set_ylabel("LL (bits / trial)")
ax.set_xticks(num_states_list)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Inspect recovered weights

The GLM-HMM fits $P(\text{Left})$ internally, so we negate the weights to
obtain $P(\text{Right})$.

```{python}
#| label: fig-glmhmm-weights
#| fig-cap: "GLM-HMM weights for K=3 model."
best_K = 3
glmhmm = models[best_K]
weights = -glmhmm.observations.params[:, 0, :]  # (K, input_dim)
feature_names = ["bias", "prevRewDir", "prevUnrewDir"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, best_K, figsize=(10, 3), sharey=True)
x = np.arange(len(feature_names))
for k, ax in enumerate(axes):
    ax.plot(x, weights[k], marker="o", color=STATE_COLORS[k], lw=1.5,
            markersize=6, zorder=3)
    ax.axhline(0, color="grey", lw=0.5)
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels(feature_names)
    ax.set_title(f"State {k}", fontsize=10)
axes[0].set_ylabel("Weight (for P(R))")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Posterior state probabilities with choice rasters

For each session we show the choice raster on top and the posterior state
probabilities below.

```{python}
#| label: fig-state-choice-rasters
#| fig-cap: "Choice rasters (top) and posterior state probabilities (bottom) for four example sessions."
posterior_probs = [
    glmhmm.expected_states(data=y, input=x)[0]
    for y, x in zip(true_choices, inpts)
]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(8, 1, figsize=(12, 10),
                         gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": [2, 1] * 4})
for i in range(4):
    ax_choice = axes[2 * i]
    ax_state = axes[2 * i + 1]
    sess_code = session_codes[i]
    sess_df = df.loc[df["session_code"] == sess_code].reset_index(drop=True)
    n_trials = len(sess_df)

    plot_choices(sess_df, shade_strategy=True, fig=fig, ax=ax_choice)
    ax_choice.set_title(f"Session: {sess_code}", fontsize=9)
    ax_choice.set_xlabel("")

    # Remove spines from the choice raster
    for spine in ax_choice.spines.values():
        spine.set_visible(False)
    ax_choice.tick_params(left=False)

    for k in range(best_K):
        ax_state.plot(posterior_probs[i][:, k], color=STATE_COLORS[k],
                      label=f"State {k}", alpha=0.85, lw=1)
    ax_state.set_xlim(0, n_trials)
    ax_state.set_ylim(-0.05, 1.05)
    ax_state.spines["left"].set_bounds(0, 1)
    ax_state.set_yticks([0, 0.5, 1])
    ax_state.set_ylabel("P(state)")
    if i == 0:
        ax_state.legend(fontsize=7, loc="upper right", ncol=best_K)

axes[-1].set_xlabel("Trial")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## State occupancy by generating strategy

```{python}
#| label: fig-state-occupancy
#| fig-cap: "Average state occupancy broken down by the generating strategy."
strategy_per_trial = df["strategy"].values
session_boundaries = df.groupby("session_code").cumcount().values

state_probs_all = np.concatenate(posterior_probs, axis=0)
dominant = state_probs_all.argmax(axis=1)

strat_state_counts = {}
for strat in ["biased", "wsls", "perseverant"]:
    mask = strategy_per_trial == strat
    counts = np.bincount(dominant[mask], minlength=best_K).astype(float)
    strat_state_counts[strat] = counts / counts.sum()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3.5))
x = np.arange(best_K)
width = 0.25
for i, strat in enumerate(["biased", "wsls", "perseverant"]):
    ax.bar(x + i * width, strat_state_counts[strat], width,
           label=strat, color=STRATEGY_COLORS[strat],
           edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.3)
ax.set_xticks(x + width)
ax.set_xticklabels([f"State {k}" for k in range(best_K)])
ax.set_ylabel("Proportion of trials")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
